title: Goeppertia lancifolia
taxonomy: Goeppertia lancifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae. This genus was previously known as Calathea and has undergone several reclassifications. Goeppertia lancifolia is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a popular ornamental plant due to its striking foliage and vibrant colors. The exact classification and scientific name of Goeppertia lancifolia may vary depending on the source.

intro: Goeppertia lancifolia, also known as the lance-leaf calathea, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Marantaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential health benefits.
